title: UnClick Ecosystem Brainmap
summary: Generated sitemap, system map, and tool-worker tree for private UnClick onboarding.

divisions.name: Admin surfaces
divisions.meaning: Private operator views and internal control panels.

divisions.name: Public surfaces
divisions.meaning: Public product, docs, marketplace, and user-facing routes.

divisions.name: Tools
divisions.meaning: MCP and gateway capabilities available to seats.

divisions.name: Rooms
divisions.meaning: PinballWake and Boardroom lanes that route work.

divisions.name: Workers and seats
divisions.meaning: Human and AI roles that move work through the system.

divisions.name: Passes and gates
divisions.meaning: Quality, proof, safety, and fidelity checks.

divisions.name: Wrappers and protocols
divisions.meaning: Thin harnesses, bridges, policies, and routing helpers.

divisions.name: Automations
divisions.meaning: Scheduled jobs, wake routes, cron workflows, and recurring checks.

divisions.name: Ledgers and proof
divisions.meaning: Receipts, audits, evidence, and proof-of-work surfaces.

divisions.name: Source of truth
divisions.meaning: Canonical state, queue, memory, and context surfaces.

divisions.name: Modules and apps
divisions.meaning: Apps, packages, and product modules that make up UnClick.

divisions.name: Launch and onboarding
divisions.meaning: Launchpad, Heartbeat, Brainmap, and first-seat orientation.

inventory.name: Control Tower
inventory.meaning: Big-job coordinator that creates worker lanes, Master Copy Box prompts, worker counts, stale takeovers, and XGate/XPass/Crews proof paths.

inventory.name: Crews Council Induction
inventory.meaning: Launchpad prompt that runs Council Lite on material work and asks for a full Crews Council only when launch, risk, mixed proof, or broad XPass evidence needs judgement.

inventory.name: Ecosystem Brainmap
inventory.meaning: Generated sitemap and system map that teaches seats what UnClick contains.

inventory.name: Heartbeat Master
inventory.meaning: Canonical schedule prompt and procedure for safe heartbeat seats.

inventory.name: Launchpad
inventory.meaning: Control hub that points seats to the next safe operating lane.

inventory.name: Proof Ledger
inventory.meaning: Structured evidence, proof freshness, receipts, and DONE trust surface.

inventory.name: JobSmith
inventory.meaning: CV, cover-letter, job application, and rules/checklist engine.

inventory.name: AutoPilotKit
inventory.meaning: Internal automation bolt-on for proof-first work motion.

inventory.name: Skills Library
inventory.meaning: Read-only starter pack of UnClick-native skills, hardwired rails, hybrid workflows, and portable skill packages.

inventory.name: UIPass Toolbox
inventory.meaning: Curated UI source registry and proof scoreboard for shadcn, Radix, React Aria, Base UI, Floating UI, Motion, 21st.dev, Magic UI, Aceternity, Origin UI, and advisory design intelligence.

inventory.name: CopyRoom
inventory.meaning: Exact-copy room for code, docs, tables, notes, and source text so seats do not retype drift-prone material.

inventory.name: FidelityPass
inventory.meaning: Checks exactness and invariant preservation when copying, refactoring, or translating content.

inventory.name: CommonSensePass
inventory.meaning: Plain-reasoning gate used before healthy, done, merge-ready, or PASS claims.

inventory.name: WakePass
inventory.meaning: Verifies ACKs, stale handoffs, and worker wake requests before motion claims.

inventory.name: Orchestrator
inventory.meaning: Continuity stream and story layer that helps seats understand what happened.

inventory.name: Memory Library
inventory.meaning: Source-linked facts, sessions, context, and generated memory shelves.

inventory.name: Boardroom Jobs
inventory.meaning: Primary work source for open, in-progress, done, and dropped chips.

inventory.name: Claude Reviewer Seat
inventory.meaning: External reviewer lane used for independent checks and proof review.

inventory.name: Codex Builder Seat
inventory.meaning: Codex lane used for scoped implementation, routing, and proof updates.

inventory.name: Cursor Builder Seat
inventory.meaning: External builder lane used for scoped code work and PRs.

inventory.name: Builder
inventory.meaning: Implements focused code or content changes from a scoped packet.

inventory.name: Coordinator
inventory.meaning: Routes work, chooses the next room, and keeps lanes aligned.

inventory.name: Improver
inventory.meaning: Turns repeated pain into system improvements.

inventory.name: Ledger
inventory.meaning: Records proof, receipts, approvals, and rollback evidence.

inventory.name: Publisher
inventory.meaning: Moves approved work toward deployment and public proof.

inventory.name: Reviewer
inventory.meaning: Checks quality, regressions, and missing tests.

inventory.name: Safety Checker
inventory.meaning: Protects secrets, auth, destructive actions, and release gates.

inventory.name: Tester
inventory.meaning: Runs proof and reports what passed or blocked.

inventory.name: IgniteOnly
inventory.meaning: Verified worker wake packets only, never build, merge, or completion state.

inventory.name: NudgeOnly
inventory.meaning: Low-risk receipt nudges that never mutate source-of-truth state.

inventory.name: SeatRelay
inventory.meaning: Stale release, smart reassignment, and bonded handoff for stuck worker claims.

inductionRows: Read standing rules, recent facts, and source-linked context before interpreting the work.
Save the accepted turn, read Orchestrator continuity, and treat it as context rather than queue authority.
Use Jobs as the source of truth for active work, proof state, blockers, and safe next action.
For big jobs, use Control Tower to split work into worker lanes, copy boxes, stale takeovers, and proof paths.
Use the generated ecosystem map to find current routes, tools, rooms, workers, aliases, and safety gates.
Route the work through the safest current Autopilot lane before acting or handing off.
Run Council Lite on material work, then prompt full Crews Council for launch, risk, mixed proof, or broad XPass evidence.
Name required PR, commit, test, CI, live, screenshot, CopyRoom, or NO_CODE_NEEDED proof before closing.
Run the focused local tests and browser or live proof that match the touched surface.
End with PASS or BLOCKER, proof link or id, cleanup state, and next safe step.
workerRows: Routes work, chooses the next room, and keeps lanes aligned.
Implements focused code or content changes from a scoped packet.
Runs proof and reports what passed or blocked.
Checks quality, regressions, and missing tests.
Protects secrets, auth, destructive actions, and release gates.
Records proof, receipts, approvals, and rollback evidence.
Moves approved work toward deployment and public proof.
Turns repeated pain into system improvements.
aliasRows: Enterprise readiness checks need a public-safe product name.
Old QualityPass references now resolve to SlopPass.
Internal worker discussion becomes a user-facing room name.
Task queue language maps to the current admin Jobs surface.
The copy policy that teaches scheduled seats how to pulse.
Low-risk receipt nudges, never source-of-truth mutation.
Verified worker wake packets, never build, merge, or completion state.
